Document:
# Part Manipulation
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Surgery"]
Part manipulation surgeries allow you to remove, reattach, or replace body parts. They are used for amputations, prosthetic fitting, species conversion, and limb transplants.
## Standard Anatomy
A typical human body has ten main parts:
[bold]Head[/bold]
[bold]Torso[/bold]
[bold]Right Arm[/bold], [bold]Right Hand[/bold]
[bold]Left Arm[/bold], [bold]Left Hand[/bold]
[bold]Right Leg[/bold], [bold]Right Foot[/bold]
[bold]Left Leg[/bold], [bold]Left Foot[/bold]
Some species have additional parts such as tails, wings, or extra limbs.
## Removing and Reattaching Parts
To [bold]remove[/bold] a limb, apply the Remove Part surgery to the target part. The limb is detached without harming the patient.
To [bold]reattach[/bold] a limb, apply the Attach Part surgery at the correct attachment site. Hands attach to arms. Feet attach to legs. All other parts attach to the torso.
## Sourcing Replacement Parts
When a patient needs a replacement limb, use a [bold]Medical Biofabricator[/bold] with [bold]Biomass[/bold]. The biofabricator can manufacture biosynthetic limbs for all species.
Every Chief Medical Officer has access to a Medical Biofabricator board in their starting equipment.
## Non-Functional Reattached Limbs
If a reattached limb is unresponsive, the nerve damage is too severe for reattachment alone to restore function. Treat the limb with Tend Wounds surgery or topical wound care until enough damage is healed for it to function again.
